adjustments: New Zealand repeatedly hit long balls downfield to Wood and then follow up for second balls
Egypt respond by looking to play through New Zealand on the counter
Egypt take a short corner before delivering into the box
key_matchups: Wood vs Egypt centre-backs / aerial duels
phases.summary: New Zealand’s early goal came from a basic set-piece delivery and poor Egyptian marking, after which the All Whites leaned into a direct long-ball game aimed at Wood and second balls. Egypt struggled to cope with that approach but found some success when New Zealand’s forward commitment left space to counter, with Marmoush forcing a save and the game becoming increasingly congested in midfield.

formations_blocks: New Zealand in a compact defensive third / low block after halftime
Egypt dominating possession and territory in the second half
New Zealand using a direct long-ball approach toward Wood
Egypt building through wide areas, especially down the right
adjustments: Salah appears to have moved into a more central role in the second half
Egypt increasing their use of right-sided combinations through Hany and Ziko
New Zealand continuing to target Wood with long balls and second-ball support from Just and McCowatt
key_matchups: Wood vs Egypt’s aerial/central defenders under the long ball
Just vs Egypt’s recovery and ball retention in midfield/left-sided transitions
McCowatt vs Shobeir on the near-post chance
Salah vs Boxall in the penalty area
phases.summary: New Zealand led at half-time thanks to a direct, efficient plan: go long to Wood and use Just and McCowatt to attack the spaces and second balls. Egypt looked slicker in possession after the restart and pinned New Zealand back, but their movement remained slow and their attacking structure still looked disjointed despite more width and a more central Salah.

formations_blocks: New Zealand retreating into a more passive defensive phase after conceding, then circulating possession along the back four
Egypt standing off a bit more once level before reasserting control between the lines
adjustments: Egypt’s wide delivery becomes a key attacking route, with Hany crossing first time for the equaliser
Ashour grows into the role of linking the lines
New Zealand slow the game by padding possession across their back four
New Zealand substitute Old on for McCowatt (66')
Salah finds space between the lines, with Marmoush drifting left and cutting in to shoot
key_matchups: Hany vs New Zealand’s defensive line for the cross to Ziko
Ziko vs New Zealand centre-backs/goalkeeper Crocombe on the equaliser
Ashour vs New Zealand’s midfield as a link between the lines
Marmoush vs Bell, who wins the ball before he can shoot
Salah vs New Zealand’s central defenders/midfield line when receiving between the lines
phases.summary: Egypt controlled possession after the restart and eventually turned that dominance into an equaliser through a wide attack: Hany crossed first time and Ziko headed in unmarked. New Zealand briefly slowed the tempo with safe back-four circulation, but Egypt remained the more threatening side, finding space between the lines before Salah finished the move for the winner after Marmoush was denied by Bell.

formations_blocks: Egypt defending in numbers / low block
New Zealand chasing with balls hoisted into the box and set-piece pressure
adjustments: Egypt used a double substitution at 84' with Salah and Ashour off for Zizo and Hossam to protect the lead
New Zealand introduced Binden and De Vries at 85' in a more direct chase
Egypt shifted to more conservative game management after the third goal, effectively 'putting the cue in the rack'
key_matchups: Trezeguet vs New Zealand's near-post marking on the corner
Randall vs Egypt's right-side defender/substitute in the 86' attack
Binden vs Egypt's penalty-area defenders on the late set pieces
Wood vs Egypt's defending against lofted crosses
Zizo vs Crocombe on the 90+3 one-on-one
phases.summary: Egypt’s third goal from a poorly defended near-post corner ended the contest as a competitive tactical phase, after which they dropped into a compact defensive shell and managed the game with substitutions. New Zealand responded with direct pressure, long throws, and repeated set pieces, but Egypt held firm while also spurning a huge late chance through Zizo on the break.
